taxonomy: Seutera wilfordii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It belongs to the genus Seutera. The plant is native to tropical Asia, specifically found in the Philippines and other parts of the region. Further taxonomic classification places it within the tribe Eritrichieae. uses: Seutera wilfordii, also known as Thai basil, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. parts: Seutera wilfordii is commonly known as the Chinese foxglove or yellow foxglove. It is a flowering plant native to China, used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
